macro_summary: Monday's US session sank as the bond rout deepened, with the 30-year auction crossing 5% for the first time since 2007, according to Reuters. US-Iran tensions escalated ahead of Tuesday's NSC vote on military options, while China agreed to address US rare-earth shortages, per White House. SPY fell 1.14%, QQQ lost 1.5%, and SOXX tumbled 3.91%.
commodities_note: WTI slipped 2% from Friday's peak to $103.31 but remains up 5.3% for the week. Gold at $4,542, down 3.7% weekly, as the stronger dollar and rising yields weigh. US10Y at 4.59%, up 3bp, with 5d move +5.3% — the inflation trade is back.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain under pressure from the dual NIM compression channels, with DBS and OCBC still hard-blocked on extension above 5% and RSI above 65. UOB exits CAUTION as its 2.6% extension and RSI 64 relax the mechanical block, but volume is nonexistent. US10Y at 4.59% keeps the key 4.20% threshold firmly above, with no MAS relief until October — the structural headwind persists for a 60th day.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired, tactically one bright spot
singapore.key_development: UOB's extension subsides below the 5% block, becoming the only Singapore name not in CAUTION — but NIM compression from elevated US10Y and SGD strength remains unresolved.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is uniformly CAUTION, with NVDA, AMD, MU, TSEM, and INTC all trapped in hard blocks — AMD and MU over +40% extended. Only TSM's 10% extension and 28 days above the 50-day keep it as the best on-deck candidate. SOXX at 508.52, down 3.9% Monday, reflects deepening caution. NVDA's earnings in three days is the only near-term re-rating event, but the setup is neutral with a 16.7% extension leaving little room for a beat-only relief.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX sank 3.9% amid the bond rout, erasing the cluster's weekend calm. NVDA's earnings on 2026-05-21 is the next catalyst — a beat must be accompanied by guidance acceleration to crack the extension block.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains cautious with AMZN and GOOG hard-blocked on extension above 11% and 18.9% respectively. MSFT edges above its 50-day but stays CAUTION as Bill Gates' complete stake exit (per Yahoo Finance, May 16) adds noise to the OpenAI concentration risk. PLTR upgrades to HOLD after its pullback removes the technical block, though its 65x forward PE is a valuation headwind. The cluster trades at a premium to the wider market, with no near-term catalysts until MSFT's July print.
big_tech.key_development: Bill Gates liquidated his entire Microsoft stake, raising governance concerns but not fundamentally altering Azure's AI demand trajectory, per analysts.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment in LITE and COHR ties optical interconnects directly to Blackwell cluster scaling; COHR is the parallel beneficiary.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10% from current levels, or if NVDA's earnings print confirms 800G optical demand acceleration.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and optical DSP demand from hyperscalers (Google, Meta) directly benefits from the same AI capex cycle driving AVGO and LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for MRVL's next earnings for data center revenue to exceed $1.5B quarterly run-rate — a signal that would pair with AVGO's strength.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Arista's high-speed switches underpin NVDA's back-end AI clusters; the photonics and 800G ramp directly increases its addressable market.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after NVDA's May 21 earnings — if networking revenue guidance surges, ANET becomes a direct AI networking proxy.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz: Ship seized east of UAE heading to Iran (per AP, May 14), NSC to vote Tuesday on military options with Saudi veto risk — oil supply disruption persists.
US-China trade: China agreed to address US rare-earth shortages and advance tariff cuts per White House factsheet (per Reuters, May 18), but chip export controls remain unresolved.
Global bond rout: 30-year auction crossed 5% for first time since 2007, fueling Fed rate-hike bets as second-wave inflation takes hold (per Reuters, May 18).
China economic weakness: April retail sales rose just 0.2% YoY, missing 2.0% forecast, while fixed-asset investment fell 1.6% — stimulus support may falter.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $103.31 and yields at 4.59% punish semis and growth stocks; SOXX is down 2.3% over 5 days. Singapore banks remain under NIM pressure as US10Y stays above 4.20%. Defense AI (PLTR) benefits but is not immune to valuation compression.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: NSC to vote Tuesday on Iran military options, with Saudi airspace access in doubt. China's rare-earth concessions announced, easing one supply-chain overhang.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions, no new entries until VIX drops below 25 and US10Y moves below 4.20%. Selective entry on oversold semis only after meaningful pullbacks; avoid energy exposure above $100/bbl.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the semis cluster is uniformly CAUTION as the bond rout and Hormuz stalemate keep risk assets under pressure. Only WRD offers a viable watch-for-entry setup.

watch_for_entry.note: WeRide at $7.14 with R:R 4.1:1 to $8.33, but volume is absent. Reclaim of $7.43 (50-day) with volume turns it actionable. Starter only — delisting risk persists.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $7.15-7.43 if volume surges above 1M shares, invalidation at $6.85.

Reassess on a pullback to $370-375 with above-average volume.
